Branch.title: Branch
Branch.description: Represents an operational branch within the DuesFlow system, enabling multi-branch management and reporting.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Branch entity.

name.description: The official name of the branch.

address.description: The physical address of the branch.

contactPerson.description: The name of the primary contact person for this branch.

contactEmail.description: The email address of the primary contact person for this branch.

contactPhone.description: The phone number of the primary contact person for this branch.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the branch record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the branch record was last updated.

Supplier.title: Supplier
Supplier.description: Manages profiles and default settings for suppliers, including contact information and credit periods.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Supplier entity.

name.description: The full name of the supplier.

contactPerson.description: The name of the primary contact person at the supplier.

contactEmail.description: The email address of the primary contact person at the supplier.

contactPhone.description: The phone number of the primary contact person at the supplier.

address.description: The physical address of the supplier.

defaultCreditDays.description: The default number of credit days offered by this supplier.

category.description: A category or type for the supplier (e.g., Raw Materials, Services).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the supplier record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the supplier record was last updated.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Stores details of each purchase invoice, including imported opening balances, for tracking payments and due dates.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

branchId.description: Reference to the Branch where this invoice was issued. (Relationship: Branch 1:N Invoice)

supplierId.description: Reference to the Supplier from whom this invoice was received. (Relationship: Supplier 1:N Invoice)

invoiceNumber.description: The unique invoice number provided by the supplier (e.g., from Tally).

invoiceDate.description: The date when the invoice was issued.

invoiceAmount.description: The total original amount of the invoice.

creditDays.description: The number of credit days applicable to this invoice.

dueDate.description: The calculated due date for this invoice (invoiceDate + creditDays).

remainingBalance.description: The current outstanding amount remaining to be paid for this invoice.

status.description: The current payment status of the invoice (e.g., 'Pending', 'Partially Paid', 'Paid', 'Overdue').

isOpeningBalance.description: Indicates if this invoice represents an initial opening balance entry.

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice data was first uploaded into the system.

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the user ID (from external auth system) who uploaded this invoice.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records individual payments made and how they are allocated across one or more invoices.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

paymentDate.description: The date on which the payment was made.

amountPaid.description: The total amount of this single payment.

paymentMethod.description: The method used for the payment (e.g., 'Bank Transfer', 'Cheque').

referenceNumber.description: A transaction reference number or cheque number for the payment.

paidByUserId.description: Reference to the user ID (from external auth system) who recorded this payment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the payment record was created.

invoiceAllocations.description: An array detailing how this payment amount was distributed among different invoices. (Relationship: Payment 1:N InvoiceAllocation)

InvoiceAllocation.title: InvoiceAllocation
InvoiceAllocation.description: Represents a specific portion of a payment applied to a single invoice, crucial for partial payment tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InvoiceAllocation entity.

paymentId.description: Reference to the Payment entity this allocation belongs to. (Relationship: Payment 1:N InvoiceAllocation)

invoiceId.description: Reference to the Invoice entity this payment portion was applied to. (Relationship: Invoice 1:N InvoiceAllocation)

amountApplied.description: The specific amount from the payment that was applied to this invoice.

allocatedAt.description: Timestamp when this allocation was recorded.

DashboardStatistic.title: DashboardStatistic
DashboardStatistic.description: Stores pre-aggregated financial statistics for quick retrieval and display on the dashboard, either globally or per branch.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DashboardStatistic entity. Can be a specific branchId for branch-level stats or a fixed ID for global stats (e.g., 'global_stats').

branchId.description: Optional reference to the Branch these statistics pertain to. Null for global statistics. (Relationship: Branch 1:1 DashboardStatistic)

totalOutstanding.description: The total sum of all remaining balances for invoices.

totalOverdue.description: The total sum of remaining balances for all overdue invoices.

upcomingDues0To7Days.description: The total sum of remaining balances for invoices due in the next 0 to 7 days.

upcomingDues8To15Days.description: The total sum of remaining balances for invoices due in the next 8 to 15 days.

upcomingDues16To30Days.description: The total sum of remaining balances for invoices due in the next 16 to 30 days.

aging0To30DaysOverdue.description: The total sum of remaining balances for invoices that are 0-30 days overdue.

aging31To60DaysOverdue.description: The total sum of remaining balances for invoices that are 31-60 days overdue.

aging60PlusDaysOverdue.description: The total sum of remaining balances for invoices that are 60+ days overdue.

lastUpdatedAt.description: Timestamp when these dashboard statistics were last updated.

definition.description: Top-level collection for managing operational branch profiles. Access is typically restricted to global administrators for creation/updates, and to authorized finance team/branch managers for read operations. Includes `branchId` as a path parameter.

Top-level collection for managing supplier profiles. Access is typically restricted to global administrators for creation/updates, and to authorized finance team/branch managers for read operations. Includes `supplierId` as a path parameter.

Central collection for all purchase invoices. Each document includes denormalized `branchId`, `supplierId`, and `uploadedByUserId` for Authorization Independence. Access is granted to users with `branchMemberships` to the invoice's `branchId` or global finance/admin roles.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Invoice document.

definition.description: Records individual payments. Each document must include denormalized `branchId` (from the context of the related invoices) and `paidByUserId` for Authorization Independence. Note: `branchId` is crucial for authorization and should be explicitly added to the Payment schema. Access is granted to users with `branchMemberships` to the payment's `branchId` or global finance/admin roles.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Payment document.

definition.description: Represents how a payment is applied to a specific invoice. Each document must include denormalized `branchId` (from the associated invoice) for Authorization Independence. Note: `branchId` is crucial for authorization and should be explicitly added to the InvoiceAllocation schema. Access is granted to users with `branchMemberships` to the allocation's `branchId` or global finance/admin roles.

params.name: allocationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the InvoiceAllocation document.

definition.description: Stores pre-aggregated financial statistics. Document `statisticId` can be 'global' for system-wide stats or a specific `branchId` for branch-level stats. Access is controlled based on the `statisticId`: global roles for 'global', and `branchMemberships` for branch-specific IDs.

params.name: statisticId
params.description: Identifier for the statistic document (e.g., 'global' or a specific branch ID).

definition.description: Dedicated collection to identify global administrators. The existence of a document at `userId` confirms global admin status, allowing for simple `exists()` checks in security rules. (Assumes a 'User' entity exists in the backend for schema referencing.)

Dedicated collection to identify members of the global finance team. The existence of a document at `userId` confirms global finance team status, allowing for simple `exists()` checks in security rules. (Assumes a 'User' entity exists in the backend for schema referencing.)

Dedicated collection for user-to-branch access control. The existence of a document with ID `{branchId}_{userId}` grants `userId` access to `branchId`. Used for `exists()` checks in security rules to ensure Authorization Independence for branch-specific data. (Assumes a 'User' entity exists in the backend for schema referencing, as BranchMembership is an implicit entity.)

params.name: branchId_userId
params.description: Composite identifier for the branch membership, combining the branch ID and the user ID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for DuesFlow is designed with paramount focus on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling, as mandated. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** To eliminate hierarchical authorization dependencies (`get()`) in security rules and support atomic operations, authorization contexts are denormalized where necessary. 

*   For `Invoice` documents, `branchId`, `supplierId`, and `uploadedByUserId` are directly embedded. This allows rules to check `resource.data.branchId` (or `uploadedByUserId`) against the requesting user's permissions without needing to fetch parent `Branch` or `Supplier` documents.
*   Similarly, for `Payment` and `InvoiceAllocation` documents, `branchId` (denormalized from the associated branch context) and `paidByUserId` are explicitly included (though 'branchId' needs to be explicitly added to their schemas). This ensures that a payment record or allocation can be validated against the user's branch permissions without costly or insecure `get()` operations.

*   Crucially, user-to-branch access is managed through a dedicated `branchMemberships` collection. Instead of using `get()` on a user's profile to find their branches, or on a branch document to find its members, a document at `/branchMemberships/{branchId}_{userId}` acts as a 'permission slip'. A security rule can simply use `exists(/branchMemberships/$(branchId)_$(request.auth.uid))` to verify a user's access to a specific branch, satisfying the 'Authorization Independence' mandate for branch-level access control.
*   Global roles (admin, finance team) are similarly managed via dedicated top-level collections (`adminUsers`, `financeTeamUsers`), enabling simple `exists()` checks in rules without relying on user profile `get()` calls for global permissions.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):** Each collection is designed to hold documents with a uniform security posture, simplifying security rules. 

*   `branches` and `suppliers` are global entities managed by administrators.
*   `invoices`, `payments`, and `invoiceAllocations` are central collections, but access is filtered by the denormalized `branchId` and `uploadedByUserId`/`paidByUserId` based on the user's `branchMemberships`. This avoids mixing publicly accessible data with private data within the same collection. 
*   `dashboardStatistics` is segregated by document ID into global statistics (`id: 'global'`) and branch-specific statistics (`id: '{branchId}'`), allowing different access rules.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure fully supports secure `list` operations.

*   For `invoices`, `payments`, and `invoiceAllocations`, queries can include `where('branchId', '==', 'userSelectedBranchId')`. Firebase security rules then validate that `request.auth.uid` indeed has permission for `userSelectedBranchId` by checking `exists(/branchMemberships/$(resource.data.branchId)_$(request.auth.uid))`. This prevents users from listing documents from unauthorized branches and ensures rules prevent unintended data exposure rather than relying on client-side filtering. This pattern ensures that any `list` operation will only return data the user is explicitly authorized to view for the specified branch.
*   Global and branch-specific dashboard statistics are queried by their explicit `id` (e.g., `/dashboardStatistics/global` or `/dashboardStatistics/{branchId}`). Rules then apply `exists()` checks on `adminUsers`, `financeTeamUsers`, or `branchMemberships` based on the requested `statisticId`.

**Invariants:** Timestamps (`createdAt`, `updatedAt`) and ownership (`uploadedByUserId`, `paidByUserId`) are included in relevant entities to support data integrity. The `status` field in `Invoice` explicitly models the finite state, enhancing predictability. Consistent naming conventions (`branchId`, `supplierId`, `{userId}`) are used throughout for clarity and debuggability.
